hard.ais_why: Avoma returned zero meetings; no conversation evidence of AI interest. Salesforce activity is RFP logistics via BuyingStation only; the buyer enforces process: 'further questions be submitted via the portal.' No contact roles, no meetings logged, no AI requirement surfaced. Low confidence.

north_star_verdict.headline: On 3 July Avedis Kabrielian emailed that Chelsea has concluded its evaluation and, whilst appreciating the offer to discuss further, is declining to progress with Zycus. This is a down-select rejection at the RFP gate: no demo was ever granted, zero discovery calls occurred, and the deal is single-threaded to a procurement administrator. Claire is now working the reply to salvage a debrief.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 May 2026 (55 days ago). Qualified 18 May. RFP issued via BuyingStation 19 May; Stage 1 submission met 28 May; Chelsea published Stage 1 down-selection w/c 8 June and contract conclusion target July 2026. Buyer replied 8 June ('still finalising evaluation'), Claire chased 3 June and 26 June, and on 3 July Avedis replied declining further discussion after concluding the evaluation. Days to close on the SF field: ~310 (12 May 2027), but that date is now moot: the RFP outcome, not the close date, is the North Star and it has landed against us. Never advanced past Formal Evaluation; never reached a demo.
evidence: Email received from Avedis Kabrielian (Procurement Manager) 3 Jul 2026: 'Whilst we appreciate the offer to discuss further, in the spirit of...' following 'our evaluation process, we wanted...' — a polite decline to progress Zycus past Stage 1.
Claire Hudson replied 3 Jul 2026 thanking the team for the evaluation and pushing back on one point directly to seek feedback and a possible debrief.
No demo was ever secured: Claire chased the demo date on 3 Jun and 26 Jun; Avedis's 8 Jun reply said 'still finalising evaluation... regardless of the outcome on moving forward to the demo stage.'

Zero Avoma discovery or qualification calls ever logged; single contact role (Rahul Rajagopal, Decision Maker, never engaged directly); no champion, no economic-buyer access, no competitive or pain intel captured across the whole cycle.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem or ROI case was ever captured. The RFP was for a 'Procurement System solution' but Chelsea never articulated the operational pain, cost baseline, headcount impact, cycle-time target or savings expected, and with zero discovery calls Zycus never heard the buyer describe it. The only implicit signal is the full-suite product scope (iContract through ANA including Merlin AI), suggesting a broad Source-to-Pay ambition, but with the 3 July decline the opportunity to build a metrics case has effectively passed; any live path now runs through a debrief that reveals what Chelsea actually weighted.
sources: SF Products__c (full S2P suite incl. Merlin)
Next Step RFP text 19 May 2026
No Customer_Business_Problem__c / Business_Objectives__c / Value_to_Customer__c populated; zero Avoma calls

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer was ever mapped or engaged. The single contact role is Rahul Rajagopal (Head Of Procurement, Decision Maker) whom Zycus never spoke to directly; the only active contact, Avedis Kabrielian (Procurement Manager), is a process administrator with no budget authority. A £150k platform purchase at a Premier League club would require finance/executive sign-off, but no such person surfaced in any Salesforce field, activity, or call. Because the buyer ran a closed BuyingStation RFP that funnelled all contact through the portal, Zycus never gained the access needed to reach or influence the budget owner, which is a core reason this evaluation was lost blind.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Rahul Rajagopal (Decision Maker, primary)
Activity contact: Avedis Kabrielian (Procurement Manager)
No X10a_Sponsor__c / Who_will_approve_budget__c / Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c evidence

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria were never disclosed to Zycus. Chelsea ran a formal two-stage BuyingStation RFP and enforced strict process ('please do submit any further questions via the portal'), so scoring weights, must-have capabilities and the AI-vs-cost-vs-fit trade-off stayed inside the portal. With no demo and no discovery, Zycus responded to a written RFP without ever validating what mattered most to the buyer. A debrief with Avedis or Rahul is now the only way to learn the criteria that decided against us.
sources: Email received 19 May 2026 (Avedis: submit questions via BuyingStation portal)
No Business_Requirements__c / AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c evidence; zero Avoma calls

decision_process.narrative: The process itself is the one well-documented element, because Chelsea published it: RFP issued via BuyingStation 19 May, written-questions deadline 26 May, Stage 1 submission 28 May, invited-only supplier demo sessions w/c 1 June, Stage 1 down-selection w/c 8 June, and contract conclusion targeted for July 2026. Zycus submitted Stage 1 on time but was never invited to the demo round, and Avedis's 3 July email confirms the evaluation has concluded against us. The process ran exactly to the buyer's published clock; what Zycus lacked was any inside voice to shape it, so the down-selection happened without warning or a chance to intervene.
sources: Next Step RFP timeline table 19 May 2026
Email 8 Jun 2026 (Avedis: still finalising evaluation)
Email 3 Jul 2026 (Avedis: evaluation concluded, declining to progress)

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics never became relevant because the deal was eliminated before Stage 1 down-selection. The only paper touchpoints were procurement-portal formalities: NDA issuance and BuyingStation onboarding referenced early in the Next Step. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review or InfoSec questionnaire was ever reached. If a debrief reopens any path, paper visibility would only matter after Zycus first earns a place back in the evaluation, which it does not currently hold.
sources: Next Step (NDA + BuyingStation onboarding) 18-19 May 2026
No contracting fields populated; deal never passed Formal Evaluation

identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain driving Chelsea's RFP was never articulated to Zycus. The full-suite scope (sourcing, contracting, procurement, supplier, risk, compliance, AI intake) implies a fragmented or manual current state, but with no discovery call and no pain fields populated, Zycus never heard the trigger, the cost of inaction, or the pain owner in the buyer's own words. Plausible pains for a club of this scale (maverick spend, contract leakage, supplier/ESG risk, slow cycle time) remain inference, not evidence, and the chance to confirm them is now limited to a post-decision debrief.
sources: SF Products__c full-suite scope
No Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c / Compelling_Event__c / What_if_this_is_not_done__c; zero Avoma calls

champion.narrative: No champion ever existed on this deal. Avedis Kabrielian (Procurement Manager) was responsive but purely administrative: he ran the RFP process, sent the 8 June 'still evaluating' holding note and the 3 July decline, and showed zero advocacy, no internal selling, no competitive intel and no coaching. Rahul Rajagopal (Head Of Procurement, Decision Maker) was never engaged directly by Zycus at any point. With no inside voice, Zycus competed as a checklist vendor in a portal-controlled RFP and had no one to warn of, or fight, the down-selection. Building a relationship with Rahul independent of this RFP is the only route to a future opening.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Rahul Rajagopal (never engaged)
Emails from Avedis Kabrielian 8 Jun and 3 Jul 2026 (process admin, no advocacy)
Zero Avoma calls

competition.narrative: The competitive field was never disclosed and remains genuinely unknown: Competitors__c is blank, no rival was named in any activity, and the closed BuyingStation RFP hid the shortlist. The 3 July decline confirms Chelsea selected a path other than Zycus for the demo round, but the winning vendor is unnamed. On a zero-call run with no fresh evidence, Zycus will not invent a competitor; the honest read is that we lost a blind, portal-run evaluation to an undisclosed field, and a debrief is the only way to learn who beat us and why.
sources: SF Competitors__c blank
No competitor named in any Task/email/Next Step
Email 3 Jul 2026 (evaluation concluded against Zycus, winner not disclosed)

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved from a live-but-slowing RFP wait to a decline in the last week: Avedis's 3 July email concluded Chelsea's evaluation and declined to progress Zycus past Stage 1, after Claire's 3 Jun and 26 Jun chases for a demo date went unanswered. Amount churned early (150k up to 350k then back to 150k on 22 May) but products stayed the full S2P suite. The verdict weakens from Slowing to Off Track on the 3 July rejection.

items.change: Avedis Kabrielian emailed declining to progress Zycus after concluding the evaluation; Claire replied seeking feedback/debrief
Buyer went silent through late June after the 8 Jun 'still finalising evaluation' note; Claire chased the demo date
Amount reset 350000 to 150000 by Anthony Gray (early CRM estimate correction; product scope unchanged)
Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation; RFP issued via BuyingStation with a published two-stage timeline

competitive_position.summary: Competition is unknown and stays unknown: no competitor is named in any Salesforce field, activity, or call, and the closed BuyingStation RFP concealed the shortlist. The 3 July decline confirms Chelsea chose to progress others to the demo round over Zycus, but the winning vendor was not disclosed. This is a zero-call run with no fresh competitive evidence, so no rival is asserted or ranked; the only way to name the field now is a post-decision debrief.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Fit was never validated with the buyer. Chelsea ran a portal-only written RFP, granted no demo, and disclosed no criteria, so Zycus never confirmed alignment on any requirement. The 3 July decline is the only fit signal: on the buyer's undisclosed weighting, Zycus did not clear Stage 1.

best_practice_check.summary: This deal was lost blind in a portal-controlled RFP; the win-strategy focus now is salvaging intelligence and a relationship, not manufacturing pipeline motion.
flags: Secure a loss debrief from Avedis or Rahul Rajagopal to learn the selected vendor, the decisive criteria, and where Zycus's written response fell short.
Build a direct relationship with Rahul Rajagopal (Head Of Procurement) independent of this RFP so any future S2P revisit is not single-threaded to a procurement administrator.
Treat the closed-portal, no-demo motion as the root cause: future Chelsea-style RFPs need early multi-threading before the portal locks contact, not a written response alone.

Avedis Kabrielian was a responsive but purely administrative RFP contact with zero advocacy, and Rahul Rajagopal (Decision Maker) was never engaged directly. Zycus ran the entire evaluation with no inside voice, which is a core reason the 3 July down-selection landed without warning. The only forward move is to build Rahul into a relationship independent of this RFP.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The AI/Merlin story was never positioned to the buyer. Chelsea granted no demo and disclosed no AI requirement, so the Merlin Intake / Merlin negotiation narrative in the product scope never reached a live conversation. AIS reads Negative (2.0) on absence of signal, not on stated resistance.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 2.0 / AIS_Status__c Negative, driven by zero Avoma meetings and RFP-logistics-only activity, not by any stated buyer resistance to AI. With no discovery call the AI appetite is genuinely untested; the full suite carries Merlin, but the buyer never engaged on it.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Chelsea concluded its portal-run RFP and on 3 July declined to progress Zycus to the demo round; we lost blind, never reaching a demo, an economic buyer, or a champion, and the only live play is a loss debrief to recover intel and the Rahul Rajagopal relationship.

win_position.text: Zycus is losing this evaluation: Avedis Kabrielian emailed on 3 Jul that Chelsea concluded its RFP and, whilst appreciating the offer to discuss further, is declining to progress us to the demo stage.

We competed blind: no demo was ever granted, the Decision Maker Rahul Rajagopal was never engaged, no economic buyer was mapped, and the closed BuyingStation portal hid both the criteria and the competing vendors.
Single-threading to a procurement administrator (Avedis) with zero advocacy left Zycus with no inside voice to sense or contest the down-selection that landed on 3 Jul.

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has reversed: after the 8 Jun 'still finalising evaluation' note, Claire's 3 Jun and 26 Jun demo-date chases went unanswered until the 3 Jul decline.
The deal never advanced past Formal Evaluation and never reached the invited-only demo round Chelsea ran w/c 1 June.

customer_commitment.text: The buyer's only commitment was to its own process, not to Zycus: Chelsea ran the published RFP clock exactly and eliminated Zycus at Stage 1 without a demo.
No Zycus-specific next step, proposal request, or pricing conversation was ever initiated by Chelsea across the whole cycle.

deal_risk.text: The primary risk has already realized: a down-select rejection at the RFP gate to an undisclosed winning vendor.
Zero discovery means no pain, metrics, or competitive intel was ever captured, so even a debrief starts from a near-blank slate.
